perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, that certain Acquisition Agreement dated the date hereof (the "Acquisition Agreement") by Valco and CMC, provides for the acquisition by CMC of certain rights, properties, assets (including a partnership interest) owned or held by Valco; WHEREAS, that certain Fee Sand And Gravel Lease dated the date hereof (the "Lease") between Valco and CMC provides for the lease by Valco to CMC of certain property containing sand and gravel deposits in Pueblo County, Colorado;
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, that certain Acquisition Agreement dated the date hereof (the "Acquisition Agreement") by Valco and CMC, provides for the acquisition by CMC of certain rights, properties, assets (including a partnership interest) owned or held by Valco; WHEREAS, that certain Fee Sand And Gravel Lease dated the date hereof (the "Lease") between Valco and CMC provides for the lease by Valco to CMC of certain property containing sand and gravel deposits in Pueblo County, Colorado; WHEREAS, a separate agreement between Valco and CMC regarding environmental liabilities will be in effect.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'WHEREAS, a separate agreement between Valco and CMC regarding environmental liabilities will be in effect' without defining or attaching that agreement, we introduce an uncertainty. Later, when the contract discusses liabilities or obligations, it's unclear whether those include the environmental liabilities mentioned here, or if they are governed solely by the missing agreement. The Non-disclosure clause will be contradicted by the abscence of any further context to this Environmental liabilities agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-disclosure (a) Except as provided in Subsection (b) below, each of Valco and Brubaker agrees that, for a period of ten (10) years from and after the date hereof, all information previously or hereafter disclosed to any of them by CMC in connection with the transactions contemplated by the Acquisition Agreement and Lease and information relating to Valco's (after the date hereof CMC's) Pueblo operations is confidential (collectively, "Confidential Information") and shall be held in strict confidence and not disclosed to any person or entity.
perturbation.justification: The added clause 'WHEREAS, a separate agreement between Valco and CMC regarding environmental liabilities will be in effect' introduces an undefined agreement. The contradicted text, section 3 which refers to non-disclosure of information regarding the transactions contemplated by the Acquisition Agreement and Lease, now has an exception with the abscence of any further context to this Environmental liabilities agreement, creating a strong in-text contradiction. This contradiction falls under the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Non-competition (a) Without the prior written consent of CMC (which may be withheld in CMC's sole discretion), for a period of ten (10) years from and after the date hereof, neither Valco nor Brubaker shall, directly or indirectly, whether as a stockholder, individual, partner, agent, representative, employee, employer, director, officer, principal, consultant, advisor, or independent contractor, or through any of the foregoing, or in any other relation or capacity whatsoever: (I) engage in the business relating to sand and gravel mining or sales of ready mix concrete, asphalt and construction aggregates, in Pueblo and/or El Paso Counties, Colorado; (ii) operate or own a concrete batch plant, aggregates operation or asphalt plant in Teller County, Colorado; or (iii) except to Valco's present customers and prospective customers in similar businesses, make any sales to any customers in Teller County, Colorado.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Non-competition (a) Without the prior written consent of CMC (which may be withheld in CMC's sole discretion), for a period of ten (10) years from and after the date hereof, neither Valco nor Brubaker shall, directly or indirectly, whether as a stockholder, individual, partner, agent, representative, employee, employer, director, officer, principal, consultant, advisor, or independent contractor, or through any of the foregoing, or in any other relation or capacity whatsoever: (I) engage in the business relating to sand and gravel mining or sales of ready mix concrete, asphalt and construction aggregates, in Pueblo and/or El Paso Counties, Colorado; or (iii) except to Valco's present customers and prospective customers in similar businesses, make any sales to any customers in Teller County, Colorado.
perturbation.explanation: By removing clause '(ii) operate or own a concrete batch plant, aggregates operation or asphalt plant in Teller County, Colorado;' we create an in-text contradiction when enforcing Non-competition clauses. Now, Valco and Brubaker, indirectly can own an aggregates operation or asphalt plant in Teller County, Colorado.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Non-competition (a) Without the prior written consent of CMC (which may be withheld in CMC's sole discretion), for a period of ten (10) years from and after the date hereof, neither Valco nor Brubaker shall, directly or indirectly, whether as a stockholder, individual, partner, agent, representative, employee, employer, director, officer, principal, consultant, advisor, or independent contractor, or through any of the foregoing, or in any other relation or capacity whatsoever: (I) engage in the business relating to sand and gravel mining or sales of ready mix concrete, asphalt and construction aggregates, in Pueblo and/or El Paso Counties, Colorado; or (iii) except to Valco's present customers and prospective customers in similar businesses, make any sales to any customers in Teller County, Colorado.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prevented Valco and Brubaker from operating or owning a concrete batch plant, aggregates operation, or asphalt plant in Teller County, Colorado. The changed text removes this restriction. The contradicted text is the same as the original text, thus the change introduces an inconsistency by allowing Valco and Brubaker to indirectly own or operate an aggregates operation or asphalt plant in Teller County, Colorado when previously they were barred from doing so. This is a direct conflict and creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (c) Valco may disclose, on a need to know basis, Confidential Information to directors, officers, employees, attorneys and accountants, subject to the last sentence of this paragraph (c). With CMC's prior written consent (which will not be unreasonably withheld), Valco and Brubaker may disclose, on a need to know basis, Confidential Information to consultants, advisors and institutional lenders, subject to the last sentence of this paragraph (c). Valco also may disclose, on a need to know basis, and subject to the last sentence of this paragraph (c), to any bona fide acquirer (whether by purchase, exchange, merger or otherwise) of the stock, of substantially all of the assets of Valco, or of the interest of Valco under the Lease, the formula under the Lease for determining the Production Royalty Rate, the historical revenues received under the Lease, the total tonnage mined (on an aggregate and not product type basis) under the Lease and total remaining tons to be mined under the Lease, and with CMC's prior written consent (not 2 to be unreasonably withheld), any other information concerning the Lease, but CMC may withhold such consent in its sole discretion as to such other information if in CMC's sole judgment a recipient of such other information is or could be expected to become a competitor of CMC in the Pueblo area. In the case of any permitted disclosure of Confidential Information under this paragraph (c), Valco and Brubaker shall inform such persons of the existence of this Agreement and take all reasonable steps to ensure that such persons comply with the provisions of this Agreement applicable to Valco and Brubaker.
perturbation.changed_text: (c) Valco may disclose, on a need to know basis, Confidential Information to directors, officers, employees, attorneys and accountants, subject to the last sentence of this paragraph (c). With CMC's prior written consent (which will not be unreasonably withheld), Valco and Brubaker may disclose, on a need to know basis, Confidential Information to consultants, advisors and institutional lenders, subject to the last sentence of this paragraph (c). Valco also may disclose, on a need to know basis, and subject to the last sentence of this paragraph (c), to any bona fide acquirer (whether by purchase, exchange, merger or otherwise) of the stock, of substantially all of the assets of Valco, or of the interest of Valco under the Lease, the formula under the Lease for determining the Production Royalty Rate, the historical revenues received under the Lease, the total tonnage mined (on an aggregate and not product type basis) under the Lease and total remaining tons to be mined under the Lease, and with CMC's prior written consent any other information concerning the Lease, but CMC may withhold such consent in its sole discretion as to such other information if in CMC's sole judgment a recipient of such other information is or could be expected to become a competitor of CMC in the Pueblo area. In the case of any permitted disclosure of Confidential Information under this paragraph (c), Valco and Brubaker shall inform such persons of the existence of this Agreement and take all reasonable steps to ensure that such persons comply with the provisions of this Agreement applicable to Valco and Brubaker.
perturbation.explanation: By removing '(not 2 to be unreasonably withheld)' we remove the requirement for CMC to have reasonable grounds for withholding consent, which may lead to arbitrary denials, affecting the acquisition process and potentially breaching the intended spirit of the agreement, creating an in-text contradiction in paragraph (c) affecting the non-disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Valco may disclose, on a need to know basis, Confidential Information to directors, officers, employees, attorneys and accountants, subject to the last sentence of this paragraph (c). With CMC's prior written consent (which will not be unreasonably withheld), Valco and Brubaker may disclose, on a need to know basis, Confidential Information to consultants, advisors and institutional lenders, subject to the last sentence of this paragraph (c). Valco also may disclose, on a need to know basis, and subject to the last sentence of this paragraph (c), to any bona fide acquirer (whether by purchase, exchange, merger or otherwise) of the stock, of substantially all of the assets of Valco, or of the interest of Valco under the Lease, the formula under the Lease for determining the Production Royalty Rate, the historical revenues received under the Lease, the total tonnage mined (on an aggregate and not product type basis) under the Lease and total remaining tons to be mined under the Lease, and with CMC's prior written consent any other information concerning the Lease, but CMC may withhold such consent in its sole discretion as to such other information if in CMC's sole judgment a recipient of such other information is or could be expected to become a competitor of CMC in the Pueblo area. In the case of any permitted disclosure of Confidential Information under this paragraph (c), Valco and Brubaker shall inform such persons of the existence of this Agreement and take all reasonable steps to ensure that such persons comply with the provisions of this Agreement applicable to Valco and Brubaker.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that CMC's consent for disclosure of certain information concerning the Lease to an acquirer 'will not be unreasonably withheld'. The changed text removes the phrase '(not 2 to be unreasonably withheld)', granting CMC the ability to withhold consent without needing reasonable grounds. This directly contradicts the earlier stipulation that consent cannot be unreasonably withheld. The contradicted text is the text itself, since a modification of the original text (that becomes the new text) is in direct contradiction to the original text.

perturbation.original_text: Each of Valco and Brubaker hereby acknowledges that CMC would lack an adequate remedy at law and CMC shall, in addition to and not in lieu of money damages, be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief against Valco and Brubaker in an action or procedure to enforce the provisions hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of Valco and Brubaker hereby acknowledges that CMC would lack an adequate remedy at law and CMC shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief against Valco and Brubaker in an action or procedure to enforce the provisions hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'in addition to and not in lieu of money damages,' we create a contradiction. While CMC retains the right to specific performance and injunctive relief, the ability to also seek money damages is removed, affecting the enforceability of breaches related to financial losses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of Valco and Brubaker hereby acknowledges that CMC would lack an adequate remedy at law and CMC shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief against Valco and Brubaker in an action or procedure to enforce the provisions hereof. Valco and Brubaker shall reimburse CMC for its expenses, including reasonable attorney's fees, incurred in connection with the enforcement of the provisions hereof relating to a breach of this Agreement by Valco or Brubaker.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that CMC is entitled to specific performance, injunctive relief, *in addition to and not in lieu of money damages*. The changed text omits the phrase 'in addition to and not in lieu of money damages', thereby removing CMC's right to seek monetary compensation. The contradicted text states that 'Valco and Brubaker shall reimburse CMC for its expenses... relating to a breach of this Agreement', implying that CMC can seek monetary damages for breaches. By removing CMC's explicit right to seek money damages, the changed text now contradicts the contradicted text which anticipates that CMC can be reimbursed for expenses related to a breach, which inherently involve monetary losses. Therefore, a contradiction exists because the changed text limits CMC's remedies in a way that conflicts with the reimbursement clause in the contradicted text.
